claude_code.title: claude code
claude_code.meta: 21 episodes · code.claude.com/docs · self-hosted clips (also on LinkedIn)

episodes.title: Your first session
episodes.desc: Launch claude in any repo, ask in plain English, and it reads your code to answer — read-only, nothing touched.

episodes.title: The agentic loop
episodes.desc: Describe a task; Claude reads, proposes a diff you approve, then runs the tests. Read, edit, verify.

episodes.title: CLAUDE.md memory
episodes.desc: One file your agent reads every session. Run /init to draft it from your codebase — stop re-explaining your project.

episodes.title: Plan mode
episodes.desc: Claude shows its plan before touching a file. Read it, edit it, approve — then it runs. No surprise edits.

episodes.title: Slash commands
episodes.desc: Built-in / commands, plus your own saved in .claude/commands/ and shared with your team via git.

episodes.title: Undo anything
episodes.desc: Made a bad edit? Rewind code and conversation to any earlier point with /rewind or Esc-Esc.

episodes.title: Parallel agents
episodes.desc: Spin up subagents that each get their own context window and tools, working separate tasks at once.

episodes.title: Hooks
episodes.desc: A PostToolUse hook runs your formatter or linter automatically on every file Claude edits.

episodes.title: MCP
episodes.desc: Give Claude live tools — databases, APIs, the browser — by adding one MCP server config.

episodes.title: Skills
episodes.desc: Package a repeatable workflow into a SKILL.md that Claude loads on demand and reuses.

episodes.title: Context window
episodes.desc: Keep long sessions lean: inspect with /context, shrink with /compact, reset with /clear.

episodes.title: Resume sessions
episodes.desc: Pick up exactly where you left off — --continue, --resume, or /resume.

episodes.title: Headless mode
episodes.desc: Pipe anything into claude -p for a scriptable one-shot answer, no interactive session.

episodes.title: @ file references
episodes.desc: Type @ to pin a file straight into your prompt — stop making Claude hunt for it.

episodes.title: /code-review
episodes.desc: Run /code-review to get a bug-focused review of your current diff before you ship.

episodes.title: Interview mode
episodes.desc: Make Claude interview you with multiple-choice questions to nail the spec before it builds.

episodes.title: /init
episodes.desc: Run /init and Claude scans the project and writes its own CLAUDE.md — no hand-editing.

episodes.title: /goal
episodes.desc: Set /goal with a measurable finish line; Claude keeps working until it is met, then stops. Research preview.

episodes.title: /sandbox
episodes.desc: Run /sandbox, pick auto-allow, and Claude runs shell commands in an OS-enforced box — no prompts. Research preview.

episodes.title: /statusline
episodes.desc: Run /statusline, describe what you want, and Claude writes the status-line script: model, branch, context %.

episodes.title: Shell mode (!)
episodes.desc: Prefix any input with ! to run a command inline — its output joins the chat so you can ask about it.

extras.title: How The Rig reviews code — meet Review-E
extras.desc: When an AI agent opens a pull request, another agent reviews it. The full review flow in The Rig — Review-E's persona, and the four gates before a change can merge. ~44 seconds.
